pattern: Count soldiers in each row (binary search exploits the sorted structure), then sort row indices by (soldier count, row index) and return the first k.
hints: Each row has all soldiers before all civilians, so the soldier count is the position of the first 0. Binary search finds that boundary in O(log n) per row.
Sort the row indices by a key of (soldier count, row index) — rows with fewer soldiers are weaker, and ties go to the smaller index.
After sorting, the first k indices in the sorted order are the answer, already in weakest-to-strongest order.

edge_inputs.description: standard case with ties in soldier count
tie at 1 soldier resolved by row index
k equals total number of rows — return all
all rows full of soldiers — order by row index only
all rows empty of soldiers — order by row index

stress.description: random binary matrices near max size with random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(2, min(size, 100))
    n = max(2, rng.randint(2, 100))
    mat = []
    for _ in range(m):
        soldiers = rng.randint(0, n)
        mat.append([1] * soldiers + [0] * (n - soldiers))
    k = rng.randint(1, m)
    return [mat, k]
